title: Drive to the UAE
description: Contrary to what you may surmise by looking at maps of KSA, you DON'T need to drive to the UAE via the Dammam road. For a few years the...
content:
### GPS Coordinates and Odometer Readings

| Location / Landmark | Odometer Reading | GPS Coordinates |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Junction 13 (Makkah-Khoreis road) - Starting Point | 0 km | 24 42.83' N; 46 45.72' E |
| Junction 18 (Leave Ring Road system) | Not specified | 24 37.98' N; 46 48.13' E |
| Junction 12 (Turn off to Al Kharj) | 30 km | 24 30.29' N; 46 56.16' E |
| Check point | 40 km | 24 12.11' N; 48 04.83' E |
| Traffic lights immediately after Al Kharj water tower | 85 km | 24 09.73' N; 47 17.87' E |
| Unusual bird houses | 114 km | 24 12.12' N; 47 33.70' E |
| Check point before Almarai’s central processing plant | Not specified | 24 13.67' N; 47 39.34' E |
| Check point before the start of the red sands | 170 km | 24 09.35' N; 48 57.14' E |
| Saudi customs post (Batha) | 520 km | 24 07.79' N; 51 34.35' E |
| Dubai Trade Centre | 1000 km (total) | 25 13.69' N; 55 17.41' E |

**Note on Haradh GPS Coordinates:** The article does not provide specific GPS coordinates for Haradh or the exact start of Highway 10's straight section, though it notes passing Nadec's arable farms near Haradh at 250 km and a check point at 260 km.

### Journey Distances and Odometer Points

*   **Riyadh to Border:** 520 km
*   **Border to Dubai Trade Centre:** 480 km
*   **Total Journey (Riyadh to Dubai):** 1,000 km
*   **Initial Southward Travel:** 100 km
*   **Eastward Travel:** 900 km
*   **Outskirts of Al Kharj:** 80 km
*   **Al Kharj Water Tower:** 85 km
*   **Left-hand fork traffic lights:** 90 km
*   **Al Safi's dairy farm:** 101 km
*   **Prince Sultan airbase entrance:** 109 km
*   **Riyadh to Hofuf cargo train parallel section:** Starts at 130 km; runs parallel for 140 km
*   **Dual carriageway construction area:** Approximately 215 km
*   **Nadec's arable farms near Haradh:** 250 km
*   **Check point after Haradh:** 260 km
*   **First "Sand Dunes" sign:** 30 km into the Empty Quarter section
*   **Batha (Border town):** 520 km
*   **UAE Border:** 5 km after the Saudi customs post
*   **Hotel location (Geoff Holland's notes):** Approximately 10 km or more from the Saudi side of the border

### Route Specifics and Regulations

*   **Maximum distance between petrol stations:** 160 km
*   **Abrupt road end distance (if Junction 12 missed):** 3 km
*   **Unsurfaced road sections:** Maximum 1.5 km long
*   **Abu Dhabi speed limit:** 100 kph (with radar warning signs at 120 kph; fines typically triggered at 139 kph)
*   **Dubai speed limit:** 120 kph
*   **UAE Car Insurance costs:** SR 100 for 10 days or SR 150 for a month
*   **Dubai Salik (Toll) costs:** Dhs 100 for sticker; Dhs 4 deducted per toll bridge
*   **UAE Exit visa fee:** Dhs 35 (or Dhs 20 as mentioned in later comments)
*   **UAE Petrol Octane Ratings:** 95 ("special") and 98 ("super")
*   **Saudi Petrol Octane Ratings:** 91 and 95
*   **Alternative Route (Geoff Holland):** Dammam road from exit 8 for 50 km, then Hofuf road; bypasses Hofuf 20-30 km before the city

### Key Landmarks and Checkpoints

*   Riyadh Eastern Ring Road
*   Junction 13 (Makkah-Khoreis road)
*   Junction 18 (Signposted Al Kharj)
*   Junction 12 (Arabic-only signpost)
*   Al Kharj Water Tower
*   Al Safi's dairy farm
*   Prince Sultan airbase
*   Almarai's central processing plant
*   Nadec's arable farms (Haradh)
*   Empty Quarter (Rub' al Khali)
*   Batha (Saudi border town)
*   Saudi customs post
*   UAE passport control office (turreted end towers)
*   Customs inspection hall
*   Insurance booths (four different companies)
*   Sheikh Zayed Road (Dubai)
*   Barsha (Toll location near Media City)
*   Emirates Road (Alternative inland route)
*   Al Wasl Road (Alternative local road)
